open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — tracked, no server impact (a user can only exhaust their own tab). Load boundaries lacking a numPages>1000 / cumulative-byte cap and a processing timeout: (1) viewer.js loadDocument() renders one canvas+thumbnail per page (linear; render-race amplifier FIXED+VERIFIED in TASK-316); (2) merge.js (TASK-319) caps each file at 50MB but not cumulative queued bytes/pages. NEW consumer this tick: watermark.js (TASK-321) re-loads the open doc's bytes into pdf-lib via doc.getData() in applyWatermark() — same in-memory class but NOT a new external boundary (it operates only on the already-uploaded, already-50MB-validated doc, and caps watermark text at 80 chars). Fix family = developer task: add a page-count/byte cap at each load boundary. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.
